1.name: Scent of the Beast
1.description: Influencing an animal by emitting the same scent
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 80-81

1.name: Sheltering Hand
1.description: Granting a little bit extra fortune
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 79-80

1.name: Touch of Life
1.description: Allows to perform minor feats of blood without spending blood (assuming the blush of life etc.)

2.name: Fortune's Blessing
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 79-80

2.name: Hide From Hunter's Eyes
2.description: Becoming invisible to the senses of animals
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 80-81

2.name: Strength of Root and Stone
2.description: Increase physical attributes without the use of blood

3.name: Breath of Life
3.description: Heal bashing damage without spending blood by using a special paste

3.name: Fortune's Curse
3.description: Cursing an enemy to suffer a minor inconvenience
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 79-80

3.name: Marking the Prey
3.description: Fixing the attention of animals to a specific target
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 80-81

4.name: Favor of the Orishas
4.description: Use substitutes (tobacco, ivory) to fuel Disciplines

4.name: Fortune's Favor
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 79-80

4.name: Guiding Spirit
4.description: Manipulate the emotional state of an animal
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 80-81

5.name: Gift of Ashé
5.description: Potions created now last the entire night instead of just for a scene, and the practitioner does not need to be present for other Kindred to use the mixtures

5.name: Mantle of the Beast
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 80-81

5.name: Smiting Hand
5.description: Cursing an enemy to suffer catastrophic misery
references: Blood Sacrifice: The Thaumaturgy Companion P. 79-80
